mechanism_narrative: Increases in the federal funds rate directly raise the discount rate for equity valuations, mechanically compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of periods where FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate equity sell-offs as recession fears materialize.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE changes are below the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series declines, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side capacity utilization, generating short-term positive momentum in equity markets as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during tightening periods, or the signal reverses direction when inflation exceeds 5%.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation crosses above 8% while the federal funds rate remains below 1%, the resulting real rate compression forces aggressive monetary tightening expectations, causing sharp negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal does not predict negative 20-day returns, or equity markets rally despite the inflation-rate divergence in subsequent months.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls directly increases aggregate household income and consumption capacity, mechanically supporting corporate revenue growth and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero correlation with 60-day forward returns, or the signal breaks down during periods of wage-price spirals.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth crossing below 1.0% annually signals severe demand contraction, prompting immediate equity repricing as recession probabilities rise and forward earnings estimates are cut.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in >55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth drops below the threshold, or the signal loses predictive accuracy in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with falling unemployment indicates a synchronized supply-labor expansion, generating strong short-term equity momentum as economic momentum indicators peak.
failure_condition: The joint indicator fails to predict positive 5-day returns, or the relationship reverses when supply chain bottlenecks dominate production metrics.
